symptoms.symptom_name: Gradual, bilateral, symmetrical hearing loss over years

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gradual, bilateral, symmetrical progression is the defining temporal pattern of presbycusis.
A years-long gradual bilateral course excludes a sudden unilateral event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor speech discrimination, worse in background noise (cocktail-party effect); difficulty with high-pitched voices

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High-frequency SNHL preferentially degrades consonant/speech discrimination in noise — characteristic of presbycusis.
Bilateral tinnitus commonly accompanies high-frequency sensorineural loss; non-pulsatile and symmetrical here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Paranoid ideation / suspicious misattribution (people whispering to exclude)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sensory-deprivation misattribution is a recognised psychosocial sequela of untreated hearing loss.
Raises but does not confirm a primary psychiatric cause; requires screening for delirium (inattention/fluctuation) and formal thought-disorder assessment to distinguish.

symptoms.symptom_name: No ear pain, no discharge, no trauma, no sudden unilateral loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pain, discharge and acute onset argues against acute infective causes.
No sudden unilateral drop excludes SSNHL at present.
Absence of true vertigo argues against episodic inner-ear vestibular pathology.
No new loop diuretic/aminoglycoside makes an ototoxic contribution unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Otoscopy — clear external canals and normal tympanic membranes (cone of light, normal landmarks); no cerumen, effusion, or discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation of clear canals excludes wax impaction — but this is a clinician-only finding, sealed; the AI cannot obtain it via telehealth.
Normal, non-bulging TMs with no effusion exclude acute middle-ear infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weber non-lateralising and Rinne positive (AC>BC) bilaterally; whispered-voice test shows profound bilateral loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-lateralising Weber with bilateral positive Rinne indicates sensorineural (not conductive) loss — clinician tuning-fork testing, sealed and not obtainable remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pure-tone audiometry — bilateral high-frequency sensorineural hearing loss

investigation_details.expected_result: Symmetrical high-frequency sensorineural loss (down-sloping audiogram), confirming presbycusis and grading severity for hearing-aid fitting
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without audiometry the loss cannot be formally characterised or a hearing aid fitted — the definitive management step is not reached.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The down-sloping high-frequency symmetrical sensorineural audiogram is the confirmatory pattern — an audiology investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician vitals — afebrile, haemodynamically stable (Temp 36.5, no fever)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile and stable argues against acute infection driving an acute change; clinician-measured, sealed.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Gradual bilateral high-frequency loss + poor speech-in-noise discrimination + bilateral tinnitus together strongly indicate presbycusis in an older adult.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes reversible cerumen impaction and otitis before attributing loss to presbycusis — the AI cannot perform it remotely and must route to in-person review.
Confirms and grades the sensorineural loss and enables hearing-aid fitting — the definitive management pathway.
Sensory deprivation carries depression and isolation risk; screen and support mood alongside hearing rehabilitation.
Not indicated for a classic gradual symmetrical bilateral presbycusis picture; would be an over-investigation. Reserved for asymmetric/unilateral or sudden loss.
